svg.text: relative to Rust (default) = 1.0 · lower = faster · 100 programs
100 programs (kata × approach) — x-order carries no meaning
Solid Rust = 1.0× is the release default — it wraps silently on overflow. Hollow rings ◌ are Rust built overflow-checked,
the same safety kāra enforces by default: median 1.05× the cost of plain Rust across the 14 overflow-exercising programs. At that matched safety kāra is ≈ 1.04× — compare kāra to the rings.
Each dot is one benchmarked program (kata × approach); there is no connecting line because left-to-right order is not meaningful. Raw numbers: bench-results.json
